text:
VLSFO's tight, alright

Haven't forgotten you, the unloved barrel. 

You're in short supply now with the medium crude trapped again in the Middle East. Demand for 0.5% fuel oil is strong too - on top of bunkers, we need you for power gen for this hot summer season too!

Doesn't help that arbs are still shut from the West, even with 0.5% E/W so high. 

I think you are headed for more strength until the crude supply situation eases.

#oott
What's the forgotten, unloved barrel? https://t.co/jbvuaWM3Sf

Refining constraints basically pushed product cracks to extreme whilst crude prices remain fairly muted.

I think we are close to that turning point now, with CPC halting and Bab el-Mandeb at risk of closure too.

My thoughts in the link below.

#oott

An oil disruption too many? 

Strait of Hormuz
Bab el Mandeb
Black Sea terminals 
Russian refineries

Memo - I'm Not That Smart, But I'm Not Going To Make The Same Mistake Twice

Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, shame on me. Valuable lessons to take away from the asinine May to July oil price action.

Free subscribers can use the one-time unlock feature.

https://t.co/1jKRZRSH8Y

Strait of Hormuz over the last 72 hours: escalation continues.

Visible vessel traffic fell off a cliff. Only the Iran lane has vessels with AIS on, nothing in the Oman lane. We know there were still transits because UKMTO reported vessels being attacked and IRGC said 4 vessels were attacked yesterday. One tanker, Kavomaleas, was hit and caught on fire. You can see that in the video time-lapse. 

The Oman lane will remain heavily contested. Bloomberg reported that tanker owners are offering 6 months' worth of pay if the crews make the voyage in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. How much is your life worth? 

I guess we will see how long this continues, but what's obvious is that this conflict remains all-or-nothing. 

Video from @MarineTraffic

Shipping disruptions in key Middle East chokepoints intensified as the US-Iran war grinds on, with vessels in the Red Sea making unusual maneuvers, while visible traffic through the Strait of Hormuz sank.

In the Red Sea, at least one vessel u-turned to head north toward the Suez Canal, while others paused their journeys, maritime-tracking data showed. The voyage shifts follow threats from the Tehran-backed Houthi militant group in Yemen to fully blockade all ships calling at Saudi Arabia’s ports.

In the Strait of Hormuz — which links the Persian Gulf to global markets — only three commodity vessels were observed transiting on Tuesday, Kpler tracking data showed. That’s the lowest daily count since early May. Early Wednesday, the waterway remained deserted, with no observable traffic.
#oott
https://t.co/2wjlL0ibjv

Tehran-backed Houthi militants in Yemen are ready to attack shipping from positions near the Bab el-Mandeb strait at the southern end of the Red Sea, according to a global monitoring body for naval security.

“Sources close to the group stated that the Houthis have completed preparations to attack shipping, including the deployment of missiles and drones,” the Joint Maritime Information Center said in an alert, published late Tuesday, without giving details.
#oott
https://t.co/Q8f4eTLelx

French shipping firm CMA CGM will impose an emergency fuel surcharge following the renewed escalation of hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz, effective August 1, it says in a notice posted on its website.

“Following the renewed escalation of hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz over the past days, fuel prices have surged sharply again, reversing the easing observed in recent weeks. As a result, bunker costs have significantly increased across all regions and trades, impacting the overall cost of ocean transportation,” the firm says.
#oott
https://t.co/az1mgGFdLd

As of July 20, total oil product stocks in #Fujairah fell to 8.492 million barrels. Overall, there was a net draw of 1.655 million barrels week-on-week. The weekly stocks movement in Fujairah saw a draw for light distillates and heavy residues while middle distillates posted a build. #oott
https://t.co/eiuQmMGeCV

The US has reportedly struck the Bidboland Gas Refinery in Mahshahr, Khuzestan Province, Iran. @HormuzLetter

The Bidboland Gas Refinery is Iran's largest gas processing facility and the largest gas hub in west Asia. https://t.co/cOSe1H2aoG
